senators.name: Chuck Schumer
senators.nerdReason: B.A. and J.D. from Harvard University. Senate Democratic Leader. Decades of deep legislative expertise across finance, judiciary, and technology policy. Known for meticulous preparation and detail-oriented legislative strategy. As chair of the Senate Rules Committee and former Banking Committee member, applies rigorous analytical approach to complex fiscal and regulatory policy.

senators.name: Kirsten Gillibrand
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Dartmouth College and J.D. from UCLA School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Known for data-driven, methodical advocacy on military sexual assault (bringing rigorous statistics and survivor data to policy reform), women's health, and national security. Dartmouth + UCLA Law demonstrates strong analytical foundation.

NY-01.name: Nick LaLota
NY-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from U.S. Naval Academy and J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. Navy officer. Georgetown Law + USNA provides strong analytical training. Career in securities compliance and state politics. Solid credentials but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.

NY-02.name: Andrew Garbarino
NY-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Villanova University and J.D. from Hofstra University School of Law. Career as attorney and state assemblyman. Legal training provides analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Hofstra University School of Law

NY-03.name: Tom Suozzi
NY-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Boston College and J.D. from Fordham University School of Law. Career as CPA, attorney, and county executive (Nassau County). CPA credentials demonstrate quantitative and financial analytical skills. Pragmatic approach to fiscal policy but not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., Fordham University School of Law

NY-04.name: Laura Gillen
NY-04.notNerdReason: B.A. and J.D. Career as attorney and Hempstead Town Supervisor. Legal and local government background. No distinctly analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NY-05.name: Gregory Meeks
NY-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Adelphi University and J.D. from Howard University School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Deep expertise in foreign affairs and financial services. Ranking Member of House Foreign Affairs Committee. Solid but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Howard University School of Law
experience: Ranking Member, House Foreign Affairs Committee

NY-06.name: Grace Meng
NY-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Michigan and J.D. from Cardozo School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Legal training provides analytical skills. Deep policy expertise on consumer protection and AAPI issues but approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., Benjamin N. Cardozo School of Law

NY-07.name: Nydia Velazquez
NY-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Puerto Rico and M.A. from NYU. Career as college professor and state legislator. First Puerto Rican woman elected to Congress. Academic background shows intellectual engagement. Deep small business and housing policy expertise but approach not distinctly analytical.
experience: Ranking Member, House Small Business Committee

NY-08.name: Hakeem Jeffries
NY-08.nerdReason: B.A. from SUNY Binghamton, J.D. from NYU School of Law, and M.P.P. from Georgetown University. Three degrees including NYU Law and Georgetown policy. Career as attorney. House Democratic Leader — the top-ranking Democrat in the House. Combines elite legal training with policy analysis expertise. Methodical, strategic approach to legislative leadership with deep institutional knowledge.

NY-09.name: Yvette Clarke
NY-09.notNerdReason: Attended Oberlin College but did not complete a degree. Career in New York City council and Congress. Deep expertise in small business and cybersecurity policy. No academic credentials or distinctly analytical policy approach.

NY-10.name: Daniel Goldman
NY-10.nerdReason: B.A. from Yale University and J.D. from Stanford Law School. Career as federal prosecutor (SDNY). Lead counsel for the first Trump impeachment inquiry — a role requiring exceptional legal analytical rigor and evidence marshaling. Combines elite education with high-stakes prosecutorial analytical expertise.

NY-11.name: Nicole Malliotakis
NY-11.notNerdReason: B.A. from Roger Williams University and M.P.A. from Seton Hall University. Career as state assemblywoman. MPA provides policy analysis training. Solid but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.

NY-12.name: Jerry Nadler
NY-12.nerdReason: B.A. from Columbia University and J.D. from Fordham University School of Law. Ranking Member of the House Judiciary Committee. Decades of deep constitutional law and oversight expertise. Led multiple impeachment proceedings. Columbia education plus 30+ years of meticulous Judiciary Committee work demonstrates sustained analytical rigor applied to constitutional questions.
education: J.D., Fordham University School of Law

NY-13.name: Adriano Espaillat
NY-13.notNerdReason: B.A. from Queens College and M.P.A. from New York University. Career in state legislature. MPA provides policy analysis training. Deep community and immigration policy engagement but approach not distinctly analytical.

NY-14.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
NY-14.nerdReason: B.A. in economics and international relations from Boston University, graduating magna cum laude. Economics degree provides quantitative analytical foundation. Known for frequently citing economic data, policy research, and CBO analyses in legislative arguments. Co-authored the Green New Deal grounded in climate science and economic modeling. Youngest woman ever elected to Congress.
education: B.A. Economics and International Relations (magna cum laude), Boston University

NY-15.name: Ritchie Torres
NY-15.notNerdReason: Did not complete college degree. Career in New York City Council. Youngest member of Congress when first elected. Deep passion for housing, technology regulation, and democracy but approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.

NY-16.name: George Latimer
NY-16.notNerdReason: B.A. from Boston College and J.D. from Fordham University School of Law. Career as attorney and Westchester County Executive. Long legislative tenure. Boston College + Fordham Law shows solid analytical training. Deep local governance expertise but approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., Fordham University School of Law

NY-17.name: Michael Lawler
NY-17.notNerdReason: B.A. from Manhattan College. Career as political operative and state assemblyman. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

NY-18.name: Pat Ryan
NY-18.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and M.B.A. from Georgetown University. Army Airborne Ranger officer with combat service in Iraq. West Point provides rigorous analytical and engineering education. Georgetown MBA adds business analytical training. Career as Ulster County Executive applying data-driven governance to local issues. Brings military strategic thinking to defense and security policy.
education: B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

NY-19.name: Josh Riley
NY-19.nerdReason: B.A. from Yale University and J.D. from Yale Law School. Career as attorney and policy advisor. Dual Yale degrees represent exceptional academic achievement. Career focused on healthcare and economic policy with evidence-based advocacy approach.

NY-20.name: Paul Tonko
NY-20.nerdReason: B.S. in mechanical and industrial engineering from Clarkson University. Career as New York State energy policy analyst and engineer for 22 years before entering politics (New York State Energy Research and Development Authority). One of the few members of Congress who worked as a professional engineer. Applies technical energy expertise directly to climate, infrastructure, and energy policy.
education: B.S. Mechanical and Industrial Engineering, Clarkson University
experience: NY State Energy Research and Development Authority, 22 years

NY-21.name: Elise Stefanik
NY-21.notNerdReason: B.A. summa cum laude in government from Harvard University. Exceptional academic credentials. Career in policy advising (Bush Institute, Paul Ryan's office) and Congress. Harvard education demonstrates strong analytical ability. However, leadership in promoting 2020 election fraud claims — a documented rejection of evidence — undermines the analytical credibility her credentials suggest.
education: B.A. Government (summa cum laude), Harvard University

NY-22.name: John Mannion
NY-22.nerdReason: B.A. from Le Moyne College and career as high school chemistry teacher for over two decades. Science teacher background provides genuine analytical and scientific methodology. Applies chemistry/science education expertise directly to STEM education, environment, and healthcare policy in Congress.

NY-23.name: Nick Langworthy
NY-23.notNerdReason: B.A. from SUNY Buffalo. Career as New York Republican Party chairman and politician. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.
experience: Chairman, New York Republican State Committee

NY-24.name: Claudia Tenney
NY-24.notNerdReason: B.A. from Colgate University and J.D. from Western New England University School of Law. Career as attorney, newspaper publisher, and state legislator. Legal and media background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: J.D., Western New England University School of Law

NY-25.name: Joseph Morelle
NY-25.notNerdReason: B.A. from SUNY Geneseo. Career as state legislator and local government official. Long legislative tenure. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

NY-26.name: Tim Kennedy
NY-26.notNerdReason: B.A. from Buffalo State University. Career as New York State Senator and transportation advocate. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.
